publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-05-02 23:59:27 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. Department of Physical Education and Athletics

did.abstract: Films and videos of Cornell University football games, scrapbooks, clippings, and programs.

container.altrender: Slim legal flat box, (10w x 15.5d x 1.5h)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070593029]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection contains films and videos of Cornell University football games, programs from Cornell football games with Yale (1950), Harvard (1951), Princeton (1952), and Michigan (1952) and "press information" booklets, various years, 1950-1975. Also a piece of the goal post from the Cornell-Dartmouth game, 1948, which Cornell won 27-26. Also programs from 1952 Navy, 1953: Colgate, Syracuse, 1954: Colgate (x 2), Princeton, Syracuse, Dartmouth, Pennsylvania. Also a music box shaped like a football (MU-3180) that plays the Alma Mater, but is out of tune. Also a digital recording of the October 10, 2015 reunion dinner of players from the 1940s. The event was emceed by Richie Moran and features the players discussing memomorable moments during their Cornell playing career. A list of players speaking is included with the recording. Also includes a circa 1940s photograph of the football team, ticket to Princeton-Cornell Foob-Ball Game, October 21, 1893. Also, "The Big Red Machine: Exciting play-by-play highlights of Cornell's 1971 Ivy League Championship season narrated by Jay Levine."
Digitization of films can be arranged if requested.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University football motion pictures, #40-2-2205. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Digitization of films can be arranged upon request.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Some films labeled "highlights" may refer to a single game.
University of Pennsylvania Archives has film of all Penn-Cornell games.
See also Cornell University Football Game Highlights, #40-2-1000, for some 1950 and 1951 reels. Also Hunter Ripley Rawlings Papers, #3-13-2843, for 1991 highlights.
See also Howard Schuck papers, #37-5-2629, for more information on the team, particularly on the Ohio game of 1939.
See also Cornell University Museum Items, #47-7-1948.
See also Big Red Band Records, #37-6-3112.

Ernest William Schoder papers, #16-4-1156 for some 1929-1930 games.
See also Cigarette Silk Collection, #6612, for a silk with an image of a Cornell football player (MU-4659).

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. Audio visual material that has been digitized: F-18, F-42-43, F-52, F-54, F-57, F-59-62, F-65-67, F-69, F-73, F-79, F-130, F-146, F-148-149, F-153-154, F-158-159, F-164, F-226, F-250, F-257, F-261, F-272, F-301-302, F-340-341, F-344, F-369, F-419, F-468, F-514-516, F-520, F-753-754, F-759-760, F-773, F797, F-2888.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Collection #37-5-3981, Cornell University Football miscellany, was merged into this collection in 2023.

unittitle.extref: Link to a copy of the printed finding aid. Use this link to see the list of all the films, over 1000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note that most films were used for practice, not broadcast. Some films were recorded by position following this pattern: reels labeled 1 = Offense, 2 = Defense, 3 = Special Teams. Other times reel numbers indicate quarters and show all plays.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: If noted, folders contain items from Arthur Mintz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Programs include: November 2, 1901; October 29, 1904 (2 copies); November 13, 1909; and Thanksgiving 1911.

scopecontent.p: Programs include: October 1, 8, 15, 22, 29; November 12, 24. Also includes a partial program from Columbia Football, November 5.

did.unittitle: Series II. Rosters, Statistics, and Notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folders include rosters, statistics, scores, schedules, and clippings.

did.unittitle: Freshman football stats and final standings
Series III. Media Guides and press packets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter about football from Frank Rhodes in the 1988 media guide.

did.unittitle: Cornell University Football History 1887-1977, compiled by Cliff Morgan
Lehigh Valley Railroad travel pamphlets for games
Cornell football hat, "Cornell Football is Red Hot"
Ben Mintz Football Golf outing T-shirt
50th Anniversary of the Fifth Down Game plastic cup
Spirit of the Red bumper stickers

container.altrender: Slim legal flat box, (10w x 15.5d x 1.5h)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070593029]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fragment of the goal post torn down by fans after Cornell's 24-23 win over Dartmouth in 1926.

container.altrender: Slim legal flat box, (10w x 15.5d x 1.5h)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924070593029]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Goal post fragment from Cornell's 27-26 victory over Dartmouth.

did.unittitle: Souvenir flag from Cornell's victory over Penn
The Big Red Machine: Exciting play-by-play highlights of Cornell's 1971 Ivy League Championship season narrated by Jay Levine, LP record. Features Ed Marinaro.
Series IX. Videos See above link for all films.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes games vs. Penn (November 4, 1988, September 29, 1989), Columbia (November 11, 1988), and Ithaca College (Steptember 22, 1989).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes games vs. Milford Academy (October 26, 1987; October 17, 1988) and Army (October 28, 1988).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 Reels. Includes games vs. Cortland (1989), Princetom (1989), Dartmouth, Harvard (October 6, 1990), and Princeton (September 15, 1990).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes games vs. Yale (1990), Darmouth (October 20, 1990), Lafayette, and Colgate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complete game, endzone camera. See DVD-712

did.unittitle: Bucknell Defense vs. Lehigh, Princeton, Penn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also F-476 in Box 63, features interviews

did.unittitle: To Help The Team Help Yourself

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Film produced by the Desenex Athletic Division. A football training film produced for coaches and trainers. Includes an intro voice over by Frank Gifford. The film discusses how to keep the team healthy and fit.

did.unittitle: Series X. DVDs Available of the following. See above link for all films.
Cornell vs. Dartmouth, 1928 (by Allan H. Morgensen)
Cornell vs. Alfred, 1936 2 of 2 (Box 22, Reel #544) Nice shots of Schoellkopf stadium crescent crowd

dao.xlink:title: Cornell vs. Alfred, 1936 2 of 2 (Box 22, Reel #544) Nice shots of Schoellkopf stadium crescent crowd

daodesc.p: Cornell vs. Alfred, 1936 2 of 2 (Box 22, Reel #544) Nice shots of Schoellkopf stadium crescent crowd

did.unittitle: Cornell vs. Syracuse, 1939, part 1 (Box 11, Reel #310) color
Cornell vs. Syracuse, 1939, part 2 (Box 11, Reel #308) color
Cornell vs. Ohio, 1939, (Reel #311, #312, #309, transferred from V-67) Shows marching band
Cornell vs. Ohio, 1939, shorter alternate version of above

dao.xlink:title: Cornell vs. Ohio, 1939, shorter alternate version

daodesc.p: Cornell vs. Ohio, 1939, shorter alternate version

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shows Cornell Big Red marching band performance and tearing down the field goal at end.

did.unittitle: Cornell vs. Ohio State, 1940, part 1 (Box 11, Reel #319) color

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes photos of the players and team roster. Amazing panning shots show a packed to capacity Schoellkopf stadium and you can see the cheerleaders and marching bands perform at halftime as well. Some of the footage in the middle is out of order.Some of the footage is out of order in the middle.

did.unittitle: Cornell vs. Ohio State, 1940, part 2 (Box 11, Reel #320) color
